name: Hypothyroidism - Lab-Only Patients (No Diagnosis, No Treatment)
description: Tricky test case: finds patients with abnormal TSH lab values but NO hypothyroidism diagnosis AND NO levothyroxine therapy (Path D). These represent untreated subclinical or mild overt hypothyroidism — patients whose labs flag the condition but who haven't been formally diagnosed or treated yet. Discoverable only by Observation queries with value-quantity filters. The hardest tricky path because the LLM must apply both a value filter AND understand that no Condition/MedicationRequest exists.
prompt: Are there any patients in our system who have elevated TSH lab values suggesting hypothyroidism, but have not been formally diagnosed or started on thyroid replacement therapy? I want to identify potential gaps in care.

prompts.naive: Find patients with high TSH but no hypothyroidism diagnosis or treatment.
prompts.expert: I need to identify untreated patients with biochemical evidence of hypothyroidism. Search Observation resources for TSH (LOINC 3016-3) with value-quantity > 5 mIU/L, then exclude any patient who has either: (a) a Condition with hypothyroidism SNOMED codes (40930008, 111566002, 83664006, 237519003, 237520009), or (b) a MedicationRequest for any levothyroxine SCD code. The remaining patients are candidates for further evaluation. This requires combining results from multiple FHIR queries with set differences.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Path D 'tricky' patients: have elevated TSH but no Condition resource and no MedicationRequest. Tests cross-resource reasoning combined with value-quantity filtering.
